# MAKKET

> The digital discovery and trust platform for Nigeria's 300+ physical markets. MAKKET helps buyers search products, find verified sellers, and pay safely. Launching 2026 in Lagos, Abuja, and Port Harcourt.

## Quick facts

- Name: MAKKET
- Website: https://www.makket.io/
- Founded: 2026
- Founder: Alex Nwoko
- HQ: Lagos, Nigeria
- Stage: Pre-MVP (active build, April 2026)
- Launch cities: Lagos, Abuja, Port Harcourt
- Markets mapped: over 300
- Tagline: "The heartbeat of every market, right in your pocket."
- Categories: Food & Groceries, Fabrics & Textiles, Electronics & Gadgets, Fashion & Clothing, Auto & Spare Parts, Building & Construction, Home & Appliances, Beauty & Personal Care, Leather & Craft, Agriculture & Farm, Industrial & Machinery, Stationery & Printing
- Contact: hello@makket.io
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/makket-hq
- GitHub: https://github.com/Makket-io

## What MAKKET is

MAKKET is the digital discovery and trust layer for Nigerian physical markets. It maps individual stalls and traders inside Nigeria's open-air markets — Balogun, Mile 12, Alaba International, Computer Village, Wuse, Oil Mill and over 300 others — and makes them searchable from any phone. Each seller goes through a 3-tier verification process (Listed → Verified → Premium). Buyers search by product, see real prices, pay via escrow, and receive doorstep delivery.

## What MAKKET is not

MAKKET is not a classifieds board, and it is not a warehoused e-commerce marketplace. MAKKET's approach to marketplace design differs significantly from the pioneer-era Nigerian e-commerce models. It is a new category: the trust and discovery layer that connects buyers to sellers operating inside Nigeria's existing physical markets — without warehousing inventory or marking up prices.

## Cities served (launching 2026)

- Lagos — Balogun Market, Mile 12, Alaba International, Computer Village, Ladipo, Trade Fair Complex
- Abuja — Wuse, Utako, Garki
- Port Harcourt — Oil Mill, Creek Road, Mile 1

## Expansion (post-launch)

Enugu, Ibadan, Abeokuta, Kano, Kaduna, Aba (Ariaria International), Onitsha (Onitsha Main), Owerri, Warri, Benin City, Asaba.
